other_observers: Original observers were non-birder friends that reported a "bittern" in their neighborhood. These observers knew I was visiting nearby and permitted me to view and photograph the bird. When it was learned that this was potentially a rare sp; species, the property owners requested that bird not be reported until much later.
specific_location: Backyard in residential neighborhood south of what once was Hwy 287 in Berthoud and east of County Rd 19. Property owners request exact location not revealed.
description: Small to medium sized heron, thick necked, heavy billed. Plumage: Overall streaked brown. Eye reddish-orange. Bill mostly dark, basal half of lower mandible light (straw colored) with distinct transition to dark distal end. Legs yellowish-green.Behaviors: Roosting in residential backyard, other (non-birding) observers stated the was attempting to roost under yard furniture and a trampoline. All of my observations/photographs were of the bird sitting on top of fences separating yards, and once on the railing of an upperstory backporch.Call: none notedPlumage: juvenile
